pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly creates and populates this file path inside the Wazuh container.
setup_task.sh and export_result.sh inject log lines like '2023-11-15 08:00:01 INFO' and '2023-12-01 12:00:00 ERROR', which exactly match this regular expression.
verifier.py uses this exact string as header_marker to count log occurrences.
scripts/task_utils.sh explicitly defines WAZUH_MANAGER_CONTAINER as this value.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on synthetically generated log data injected into a Wazuh container ('wazuh-wazuh.manager-1'). The target file is confirmed to be '/var/log/billing_app.log'. The log timestamp format correctly uses the pattern '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2} \d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}', matching the dummy data injected by setup scripts. The expected archive log header pattern is verified as '->/var/log/billing_app.log', which is strictly checked by the verifier to ensure proper event merging.
